---
title: "Endangered Languages of the World"
subtitle: "INFO 526 - Summer 2026 - Final Project"
author: "Damon Kelson"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: images/free_german_text.jpg
  data-background-size: stretch
  data-background-opacity: "0.2"
  data-slide-number: none
format:
  revealjs:
    theme:  ['data/customtheming.scss']
  
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| include: false

# Load packages here
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, kableExtra, forcats, sf, ggrepel,
               patchwork, cowplot, rnaturalearth, rnaturalearthdata)

# I'm replacing the pacman load because the git website publishing is failing due to not loading via pacman
# Hopefully this explicit method fixes the publishing issue
#library(tidyverse)
#library(kableExtra)
#library(forcats)
#library(sf)
#library(ggrepel)
#library(patchwork)
#library(rnaturalearth)
#library(rnaturalearthdata)

# The above didn't work, I've reverted to pacman but may try to see if I can get this working

```

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

# Plot theme, attempting to globally reduce empty space
theme_set(
  theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(0,0,0,0)
    )
)
```


```{r Initial csv assignment and prep}
languages <- read_csv("data/languages.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
status    <- read_csv("data/endangered_status.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
families  <- read_csv("data/families.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

status_order <- status |>
  distinct(status_code, status_label) |>
  arrange(status_code) |>
  pull(status_label)
```

```{r group and family prep}
unclear_groups <- c("Bookkeeping", "Unclassifiable", "Pidgin",
                     "Speech Register", "Sign Language", "Unattested",
                     "Artificial Language", "Mixed Language")
# ~10% of the data set!

# language count per family
family_size <- languages |>
  filter(!is.na(family_id)) |>
  count(family_id, name = "family_size")
```

```{r Base data}
# Base combined data set
lang_base <- languages |>
  left_join(status, by = "id") |>
  left_join(families, by = c("family_id" = "id")) |>
  left_join(family_size, by = "family_id") |>
  mutate(
    status_label = factor(status_label, levels = status_order, ordered = TRUE),
    # 3-category split: 1 | 2,3,4,5 | 6... NA kept around (~5%)
    risk_group = case_when(
      status_code == 1     ~ "not endangered",
      status_code %in% 2:5 ~ "at risk",
      status_code == 6     ~ "extinct"
    ),
    risk_group = factor(risk_group, levels = c("not endangered", "at risk", "extinct")),
    is_unclear_group = family %in% unclear_groups,
    family_bucket = case_when(
      is_isolate         ~ "isolate",
      is_unclear_group    ~ "unclear (exclude)",
      family_size <= 10   ~ "small (2-10)",
      family_size <= 50   ~ "medium (11-50)",
      family_size > 50    ~ "large (50+)"
    ),
    family_bucket = factor(family_bucket, levels = c(
      "isolate", "small (2-10)", "medium (11-50)", "large (50+)",
      "unclear (exclude)"
    ))
  )
```

```{r Map prep}
lang_geo <- lang_base |> filter(!is.na(latitude), !is.na(longitude))

# country polygons
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf")

# map with extinction dropped
map_data_excl_extinct <- lang_geo |>
  filter(risk_group %in% c("not endangered", "at risk")) |>
  mutate(at_risk_num = as.numeric(risk_group == "at risk"))

# per family risk severity breakdown
family_severity <- lang_base |>
  filter(!is.na(risk_group), family_bucket != "unclear (exclude)") |>
  count(family_bucket, risk_group) |>
  group_by(family_bucket) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(family_bucket = fct_drop(family_bucket))
```

```{r hex refinement prep}
# ECK4 equal-area pseudocyrlindrical projection
# initiated before grid cells to preserve equal area across latitudes
eck4 <- "+proj=eck4"

lang_sf    <- st_as_sf(map_data_excl_extinct, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326) |>
  st_transform(eck4)
world_proj <- st_transform(world, eck4)
```

```{r hex definitions}
# projected CRS, cellsize in meters, hexagon shape
# cellsize tuning for hexplot
grid <- st_make_grid(world_proj, cellsize = 900000, square = FALSE) |>
  st_as_sf() |>
  mutate(cell_id = row_number())

# per cell % at risk, n_lang
cell_summary <- st_join(lang_sf, grid) |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  group_by(cell_id) |>
  summarize(pct_at_risk = mean(at_risk_num), n_lang = n(), .groups = "drop")

# drop empty cells, constrain representations of large differences
grid_plot <- grid |>
  left_join(cell_summary, by = "cell_id") |>
  filter(!is.na(n_lang))
```


```{r isolate color control}
# bar chart + region map shared colors 
risk_colors <- c(
  "not endangered" = "#2A6F97",
  "at risk"         = "#E9A23B",
  "extinct"         = "#B23A48"
)
```

```{r isolates bar prep}
# bar chart family labeling
family_severity <- family_severity |>
  mutate(
    label_size = if_else(risk_group == "extinct", 6, 4),
    label_face = if_else(risk_group == "extinct", "bold", "plain")
  )

p_bar <- ggplot(family_severity, aes(x = family_bucket, y = n, fill = risk_group)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill", width = 0.7, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 1), size = label_size, fontface = label_face,
        group = risk_group),
    position = position_fill(vjust = 0.5), color = "white"
  ) +
  scale_size_identity() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = risk_colors) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = NULL, caption = "Glottolog 5.2.1 (via TidyTuesday, 2025-12-23)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "cm"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 18)
)
```

```{r isolate prep 2}
iso_geo <- lang_base |> filter(is_isolate, !is.na(latitude), !is.na(longitude))
iso_sf  <- st_as_sf(iso_geo, coords = c("longitude", "latitude"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)

# map crops/isolate regions, also used in calculations
sa_box <- list(lon = c(-100, -20), lat = c(-35, 22))
oc_box <- list(lon = c(110, 175), lat = c(-45, 5))
```

```{r isolate regions}
# isolate calculation for combined region percent of all isolates, fun modular gimmick!
iso_region <- iso_sf |>
  mutate(
    region_focus = case_when(
      between(latitude, sa_box$lat[1], sa_box$lat[2]) & between(longitude, sa_box$lon[1], sa_box$lon[2]) ~ "Northern South America",
      between(latitude, oc_box$lat[1], oc_box$lat[2]) & between(longitude, oc_box$lon[1], oc_box$lon[2]) ~ "Australia, New Guinea & Oceania",
      TRUE ~ "elsewhere"
    )
  ) |>
  st_drop_geometry()
```

```{r isolate plots}
p_map_base <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world, fill = "grey93", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(data = iso_geo, aes(longitude, latitude, color = risk_group), alpha = 0.7,
             size = 4, position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.2, seed = 1)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = risk_colors) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

p_sa <- p_map_base + coord_sf(xlim = sa_box$lon, ylim = sa_box$lat, expand = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Northern South America",
    caption = "Glottolog 5.2.1 (via TidyTuesday, 2025-12-23)",
    color = "Endangerment Status") +
  theme(
    legend.position = c(0.98, 0.98),
    legend.justification = c(1, 1),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.25, "cm"),
    legend.key.width = unit(0.8, "cm"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.35, "cm"),
    legend.margin = margin(5,5,5,5),
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5),
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16, margin = margin(b = 30)
    ),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10)
    )
p_oc <- p_map_base + coord_sf(xlim = oc_box$lon, ylim = oc_box$lat, expand = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Australia, New Guinea & Oceania"
    ) + 
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16, margin = margin(b = 30))
        )
```

```{r isolate callout}
focus_pct <- mean(iso_region$region_focus != "elsewhere")

# Section needs constant revising... think about scaling choices
p_callout <- ggplot() +
  # playing around with use of slide heading instead of title/annotates
#  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0.65, label = scales::percent(focus_pct, accuracy = 1),
#           size = 9, fontface = "bold") +    # headline stat
#  annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0.25, size = 3.2,   # caption adj 
#           label = "of all isolates worldwide sit in northern South America
#           or Australia, New Guinea & Oceania") +
  xlim(-1, 1) + ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2))
```

## Dataset & Analysis Questions

<div style="font-size: 36px; line-height: 1.4;">

* Dataset: Glottolog 5.2.1 (TidyTuesday)
  * ~8,000 documented languages.
  * Geographic coordinates, language families, endangerment status, isolate status...
  
* Analysis Questions:
  * How does geography correlate with endangered language status?
  * Are language isolates and small language families more vulnerable?
</div>

## Endangerment Risk by Geographic Macroarea

::: {.fill}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="70%"}

```{r macroarea prep}
country_macro <- map_data_excl_extinct |>
  filter(!is.na(macroarea), !str_detect(macroarea, ";"), !is.na(countries)) |>
  separate_longer_delim(countries, delim = ";") |>
  count(countries, macroarea) |>
  group_by(countries) |>
  slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup()

# Uruguay parses as "Eurasia" due to heavy presence of foreign languages
# This exists to explicitly correct the above for the only failure I can find
country_macro <- country_macro |>
  mutate(
    macroarea = if_else(countries == "UY", "South America", macroarea)
  )

macro_summary <- map_data_excl_extinct |>
  filter(!is.na(macroarea), !str_detect(macroarea, ";")) |>
  group_by(macroarea) |>
  summarize(pct_at_risk = mean(at_risk_num), n_lang = n(), .groups = "drop")

country_risk <- world |>
  left_join(country_macro, by = c("iso_a2_eh" = "countries")) |>
  left_join(macro_summary, by = "macroarea")
```

```{r macroarea plot}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7
#| out-width: 100%
#| fig-align: center


ggplot(country_risk) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = pct_at_risk), color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.15) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
  low = "grey90",
  high = "#C0392B",
  labels = scales::percent,
  na.value = "white",
  guide = guide_colorbar(
    barwidth = unit(10, "cm"),
    barheight = unit(0.7, "cm"),
    title.position = "top",
    title.hjust = 0.5
)
) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
  legend.position = "bottom",
  legend.title = element_text(size = 16),
  legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
  axis.title = element_blank(),
  axis.text = element_blank(),
  axis.ticks = element_blank(),
  plot.caption = element_text(size = 10)
) +
  labs(title = NULL, caption = "Glottolog 5.2.1 (via TidyTuesday, 2025-12-23)", fill = "% of languages at risk")
```

:::

::: {.column width="30%"}

<br>

```{r macro area table}
macro_summary |>
  arrange(desc(pct_at_risk)) |>
  transmute(Region = macroarea,
            `% at risk` = scales::percent(pct_at_risk, accuracy = 1),
            `Number of Languages` = n_lang) |>
  kable() |>
  kable_styling(font_size = 16)
```

:::

::::

:::

## Regional Patterns of Language Endangerment


```{r hex plot}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7
#| out-width: 100%
#| fig-align: center


ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = world_proj, fill = "grey97", color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.15) +
  geom_sf(data = grid_plot, aes(fill = pct_at_risk), color = NA) +
  # border coastline redraw for clarity
  geom_sf(data = world_proj, fill = NA, color = "grey10", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "grey90", high = "#C0392B", labels = scales::percent,
  guide = guide_colorbar(
    title.position = "top",
    title.hjust = 0.5,
    barwidth = unit(10, "cm"),
    barheight = unit(0.7, "cm")
)) +
  coord_sf(crs = eck4) +
  labs(title = "Language risk summarized in equal-area hexagons", caption = "Glottolog 5.2.1 (via TidyTuesday, 2025-12-23)", fill = "% of languages at risk") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
   legend.position = "bottom",
   legend.title = element_text(size = 12),
   legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
   axis.title = element_blank(),
   axis.text = element_blank(),
   axis.ticks = element_blank(),
   plot.caption = element_text(size = 10),
   plot.title = element_text(size = 20)
)
```



## Language Endangerment <br>by Family Size



```{r bar chart slide}
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7
#| out-width: 100%
#| fig-align: center

p_bar
```



## `r scales::percent(focus_pct, accuracy = 1)` of Language Isolates are <br>Concentrated in Two Regions

::: {.fill}

```{r isolate regions side by side}
(p_sa ) |
(p_oc + theme(
    legend.position = "none"
))

# Legend/titles looks out of proportion below, but its specifications match desired rendered presentation behavior
```

:::


## Conclusions & Limitations

<div style="font-size: 32px; line-height: 1.3;">

* Highest proportions of endangered languages occur across the Americas and Papunesia.
* Only 3% of isolates are not endangered, 44% already extinct

However...

* Geographic coordinates are approximate, not unimpeachably robust
* Endangerment status categories hide deeper nuances
* Complex web of causative factors, no simple narrative can adequately describe all scenarios
* Limited data: no extinction data approximations, "unclear" groups separated early in analysis

</div>
